steps.title: Map the sentence structure
steps.content: Before the blank: "The vessel took six days to dislodge." The phrase "in part due to its sheer size" is a parenthetical reason attached to that clause. After the blank: "it’s as heavy as two thousand blue whales when fully loaded" — a full independent clause that explains what "sheer size" means.

steps.title: Decide the punctuation
steps.content: The parenthetical "in part due to its sheer size" needs a comma on its left side. Between the two independent clauses, a comma alone would be a splice; a colon works because the second clause elaborates on "sheer size."

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — dislodge in part due to its sheer size,: no comma before the parenthetical, and uses a comma between two independent clauses (comma splice). ✗
  • Choice B — dislodge, in part due to its sheer size:: opens the parenthetical with a comma and uses a colon to introduce the elaboration about weight. ✓
  • Choice C — dislodge, in part due to its sheer size,: still joins two independent clauses with only a comma — comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — dislodge, in part, due to its sheer size: brackets "in part" with commas but leaves no punctuation at all between the two independent clauses — run-on. ✗
B is correct.
